_metadata.description: Compiled Mario Kart Wii physics values, constants, and data tables
sources: jdaster64 GameFAQs FAQ (exact top speeds, acceleration formulas)
GameFAQs MKWii detailed stats WIP thread
mkw-structures (reverse-engineered C headers by stebler, SwareJonge, 1superchip, CLF78 — gravity=1.3, speed fields, drift states, boost system)
_metadata.confidence_notes: Top speed values in km/h are well-established from reverse engineering. Internal game units (floating point) are less certain and marked accordingly. Item probabilities are exact from game data. Mini-turbo durations are approximate from community testing.

speed.description: Maximum top speed values in km/h. Internal game speed is stored as a floating-point value in game units.
speed.note: The game runs at 60 FPS (NTSC). Speed in km/h can be converted from internal units: approximately 1 internal unit ≈ 1 km/h for display purposes, though the actual internal representation uses fixed-point or float math.

bikes.note: Bikes have a wider speed range. Inside-drifting bikes tend to have higher top speeds than outside-drifting ones at the top end.

top_speeds_by_vehicle.source: Compiled from jdaster64 FAQ and community testing. Some values may vary by ±1 kph due to rounding.

weight_class_speed_bonus.description: Character weight class modifies vehicle base stats. The in-game speed bar is a composite of vehicle base + character bonus.

acceleration.description: Acceleration determines how quickly a vehicle reaches its top speed. The acceleration curve in MKWii is non-linear.
acceleration.note: Acceleration is measured as time to go from 0 to 60 kph in seconds. The internal acceleration formula is non-trivial: at lower speeds, acceleration is lower, peaks in the middle range, and decreases as you approach top speed.

mini_turbo.description: Mini-turbos are speed boosts obtained from drifting. MKWii has 3 stages of mini-turbo for karts (blue, orange/red), and 2 stages for bikes (blue, orange).

duration_kart.source: GameFAQs Blue vs Orange MT thread

orange_mini_turbo.available_to: Karts and bikes (both can achieve orange)

duration_kart.note: Karts can achieve the super mini-turbo (red sparks). Bikes cannot.

red_super_mini_turbo.available_to: Karts only (bikes cannot get red sparks)

speed_effect.description: Mini-turbo provides a speed boost above the vehicle's normal top speed. The boost speed is determined by the mini-turbo stat and the vehicle's speed stat.
speed_effect.note: The exact speed multiplier varies by combo. A rough estimate is 1.1-1.3x normal top speed during the boost.

standing_mini_turbo.description: In MKWii, you can charge a mini-turbo while stationary. When released, it accelerates you back to top speed instantly. This is a well-known mechanic.

handling_and_steering.description: Handling determines the vehicle's turning radius. Steering is the responsiveness of turning input.
handling_and_steering.note: Internal handling values affect turning rate per frame. Higher handling = tighter turns. The stat is a composite of vehicle base + character bonus.

drift_type.karts: Outside drift (drift outward, then snap inward)

off_road.description: Off-road stat determines how much speed is maintained when driving on off-road surfaces (grass, sand, etc.).

speed_retention_range.note: A multiplier of 0.400 means the vehicle retains 40% of its normal speed off-road.

example.vehicle_with_0.400: At top speed of 80 kph, off-road speed would be 32 kph
example.vehicle_with_0.185: At top speed of 80 kph, off-road speed would be 14.8 kph

weight.description: Weight affects collision outcomes (who gets bumped), off-road performance, and slight stat bonuses.

drift_stat.description: Drift affects how the vehicle handles during a drift and how quickly mini-turbos charge. A higher drift stat means the vehicle turns more sharply during a drift.
drift_stat.note: The drift stat interacts with steering and handling to determine drift turning radius.

gravity.description: The game uses a gravity value for physics calculations (jumps, slopes, falling).
gravity.address_source: mariokartwii.com Dynamic Gravity code by JoshuaMK
gravity.note: Found in PlayerPhysics.gravity field (offset 0x130 in the struct). Comment says '1.3f most of the time'. This is the primary gravity constant used in the physics update loop.

hop_gravity.description: Separate gravity value used during hops/jumps
hop_gravity.note: May differ from the main gravity value for more satisfying jump arcs

friction.description: Surface friction values are defined per-surface-type in the KCL (collision) file for each track.
friction.note: Friction is not a global constant but per-surface. The KCL file contains type IDs that map to surface properties including friction, off-road penalty, and trick availability.

known_surface_types.off_road: Grass/sand/dirt, speed reduced by off-road stat
known_surface_types.trickable: Off-road surface that allows trick jumps
known_surface_types.source: TCRF wiki (Unused Collision Type Effects), community knowledge

kcl_type_codes.source: TCRF wiki, Wiimms SZS Tools documentation

engine_class_speed_multiplier.description: Speed multiplier applied based on engine class (cc). Stored in PlayerSub10.speedMultiplier.

drift_states.description: Internal drift state machine values from PlayerSub10.driftState

surface_property_flags.description: Bit flags in PlayerSub18.surfaceProperties for floor collision detection

speed_fields.description: Internal speed fields in PlayerSub10 struct
speed_fields.softSpeedLimit: Soft speed cap (slight drag above this)
speed_fields.vehicleSpeed: Current actual speed of the vehicle
speed_fields.speedDragMultiplier: Drag factor applied when exceeding soft limit
speed_fields.kclSpeedFactor: Speed multiplier from KCL surface type
speed_fields.kclRotFactor: Rotation multiplier from KCL surface type

internal_speed_unit.description: Internal speed is stored as floating-point in PlayerSub10 fields. Speed factors from KCL (speedFactor, rotFactor in CollisionData) multiply the base values per frame.

rakshi_wheel_rotation.description: The rakshi (spinning wheel) on the menu screen rotates based on the vehicle's top speed. This is a well-known visual indicator.

item_probabilities.description: Item probability tables determine what item a player receives from item boxes. Probabilities depend on: player position (1st-12th), number of players (2-12), and race type (VS race, Grand Prix, Battle).
item_probabilities.position_dependency: Probabilities are heavily weighted by position - 1st place gets mostly weak items, last place gets powerful items
item_probabilities.note: Full tables are too large to include here. See mariowiki.com/Mario_Kart_Wii_item_probability_distributions for complete tables.
sources: mariowiki.com - Full item probability distribution tables

notable_rules.first_few_seconds: Power items (Bullet Bill, Golden Mushroom, Star, Thunder Cloud) are not available in the first few seconds of a race
notable_rules.cooldown: Some items have a cooldown period after use before they can appear again
notable_rules.max_counts: There is a maximum number of each item type that can exist simultaneously in a race

approximate_distribution_example_8p.description: Approximate item probabilities for an 8-player VS race. Values are rough averages for illustration; exact values vary by position.

character_stat_bonuses_summary.description: Each character contributes a small bonus (or penalty) to each stat based on their weight class. The total stat for any combo = vehicle base stat + character bonus.
character_stat_bonuses_summary.source: jdaster64 GameFAQs FAQ, Super Mario Files

vehicle_stats_base.description: Base stat values for all 36 vehicles. Stats are on the game's internal scale. Character bonuses are added to these.

all_36_vehicles.source_note: Base stat values from IGN guide, SportDoggy.com, and jdaster64 FAQ. Individual point values may have minor rounding differences between sources.

kinoko_project.description: Kinoko is a C++ reimplementation of MKWii's physics engine by vabold (Aiden). It achieves frame-perfect ghost replay accuracy on all 32 original tracks and many custom tracks.
kinoko_project.status: All 64 test cases (32 original + 32 no-shortcut) pass with 100% frame accuracy
kinoko_project.note: The Kinoko source code would contain the exact internal physics constants, but the repo may be private or the relevant source files were not accessible at time of compilation. Worth checking directly for exact float values.
kinoko_project.related_project: stblr/Hanachan (earlier physics reimplementation that Kinoko builds upon)
